methods.description: Adaptation of the FISH protocol for in situ hybridization on foraminifera, based on existing protocols for marine prokaryotes and other microbial eukaryotes.

steps.description: All foraminifera were picked up from the cultures, prepared in our laboratory, and/or from sediment samples.
steps.searchable_summary: collect foraminifera samples from cultures and sediment

steps.description: Specimens were considered as alive based on cytoplasm colour, presence of pigments, and/or pseudopodial activity under a microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: determine viability of foraminifera specimens using microscopy

steps.description: To test the efficiency of FISH over different foraminiferal taxonomic groups, we selected specimens of Ammonia and of the Miliolidae group.
steps.searchable_summary: select foraminifera specimens for FISH efficiency testing

steps.description: Analyses were also performed on agglutinated species, such as Leptohalysis scottii and Eggerella scabra.
steps.searchable_summary: perform FISH analysis on agglutinated foraminifera species

steps.description: To optimize the procedure, we compared two oligonucleotide probes: Euk1209R and S17.
steps.searchable_summary: compare oligonucleotide probes for FISH optimization

methods.description: Collection and initial processing of sediment samples from two shallow water sites in the Central Adriatic Sea.

steps.description: Sediments were collected from two shallow water sites (Falconara and Portonovo) using a Van Veen grab.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using Van Veen grab

steps.description: Only the top 2 cm of the sediment, containing the highest abundance of living foraminifera, was collected.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate top 2 cm of sediment for foraminifera abundance

steps.description: The sediment was stored into a plastic box filled with seawater and carried to the laboratory within 2-4 h.
steps.searchable_summary: store and transport sediment samples in seawater

steps.description: Biochemical conditions of the Falconara and Portonovo sites were characterized by chlorophyll-a, phaeopigments, proteins, and biopolymeric carbon concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: characterize biochemical conditions of sediment sites
